name: Els Estrets d'Arnes and Toll Blau
name_original: Els Estrets d’Arnes y el Toll Blau
description:
The Estrets d'Arnes are located within the Els Ports Natural Park and are one of the natural wonders of Catalonia. The Estrets River runs through the center of the impressive gorge with its vertical walls, forming pools where swimming is not permitted.

The only pool along the route where swimming is permitted is the Toll Blau, located right at the end of the trail. Shallow and located in an incredible setting, it is the best reward after the hike.

The route is not difficult at all and begins at the La Franqueta recreational area. You can park your vehicle here. Please note that the city council restricts access in summer, so you should find out before you go: https://parcsnaturals.gencat.cat/ca/xarxa-de-parcs/ports/gaudeix-del-parc/equipaments-i-itineraris/itineraris-arees-lleure/franqueta-horta-sant-joan/
region_intro: Hiking around Horta De Sant Joan offers diverse terrain within the Terra Alta region, characterized by imposing mountains, olive groves, almond trees, and vineyards. The area encompasses a significant portion of the Els Ports Natural Park, featuring gorges, waterfalls, and varied landscapes. Hikers can explore paths ranging from accessible routes to challenging trails through narrow gorges with towering rock walls, distinctive rock towers like Roques de Benet, and pyramid-shaped mountains. The region also includes remote mountainous areas with sparse ridges, mountain forests, and springs. Best hiking trails around Horta De Sant Joan The most popular hiking route is Els Estrets d'Arnes and Toll Blau, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through narrow gorges and past a picturesque blue pool. Another top favourite among local hikers is Estels del Sud Trail, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.6 km) path. This longer route involves significant elevation gain, traversing varied terrain within the natural park. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Roca Grossa – Rocky Scree Path loop from Roques de Benet, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through the distinctive Roques de Benet landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Horta De Sant Joan is defined by mountainous massifs, deep gorges, and ancient railway lines converted into greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult ascents. The routes in Horta De Sant Joan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Horta De Sant Joan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Horta de Sant Joan?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Horta de Sant Joan, offering a wide range of options from easy walks to challenging ascents within the stunning Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Yes, Horta de Sant Joan offers several accessible paths suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1734224086/els-estrets-d-arnes-and-toll-blau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets d'Arnes and Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, is a moderate trail that leads through picturesque gorges and past a blue pool, which can be enjoyable for older children. The Via Verda (Greenway) de la Terra Alta also provides a flat, paved option perfect for all ages.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Many trails in Horta de Sant Joan and the surrounding Els Ports Natural Park are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas, to respect wildlife and other hikers. Be sure to check specific regulations for the Els Ports Natural Park before you go.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic gorges of Els Estrets d'Arnes, the distinctive rock formations of Roques de Benet, or the pyramid-shaped Muntanya de Santa Bàrbara. The area also features beautiful waterfalls and pools, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Roca Grossa – Rocky Scree Path loop from Roques de Benet&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience the unique landscape of the Roques de Benet and return to your starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: The best times to hike in Horta de Sant Joan are typically spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winters are generally mild, but higher elevations in Els Ports Natural Park can experience colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951808240/estels-del-sud-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estels del Sud Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a difficult 21.6 km path with significant elevation gain, traversing varied terrain within the natural park. Other challenging options include routes around the Roques de Benet, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benet Rocks – El Castell Summit loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: While Horta de Sant Joan is accessible by bus from larger towns, direct public transport to specific trailheads within the Els Ports Natural Park can be limited. It's often more convenient to reach trail starting points by car, especially for routes deeper within the park. Check local bus schedules for connections to Horta de Sant Joan itself.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Horta de Sant Joan village. For popular trailheads like those leading to Els Estrets d'Arnes or Roques de Benet, designated parking areas are usually provided. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: The hiking experiences in Horta de Sant Joan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from narrow gorges and distinctive rock formations to serene olive groves, and the well-preserved natural environment of the Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming after a hike?
A: Yes, the region offers spots for refreshing dips. The 'Water Route to Les Olles' on the Canaletes River leads to popular bathing spots with gorges and pools. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;, mentioned in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1734224086/els-estrets-d-arnes-and-toll-blau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets d'Arnes and Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt; route, is also known for its picturesque blue pool where you can cool off.

Q: Are permits required for hiking in Els Ports Natural Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for standard hiking on marked trails within Els Ports Natural Park. However, for certain activities like canyoning or accessing very sensitive areas, specific permissions might be necessary. Always check the official park regulations if you plan activities beyond regular hiking.
tips:
The La Franqueta recreational area is located next to the Els Estrets river. It is perfect for having a picnic and enjoying nature. 

Keep in mind that the responsible town councils limit vehicle access in the busiest months to avoid overcrowding. Find out before you go.
The Estrets d'Arnes path is a true natural wonder surrounded by imposing rock walls. Without a doubt, it is one of the most spectacular mountain sites in all of Catalonia.
El Toll Blau is the only pool in which bathing is allowed on the entire Camino de les Estrets.
